source_jobs.title: Product Engineer (UI-Focused) — Horizon
source_jobs.description:
PREFECT https://www.prefect.io/ BUILDS AND OPERATES RESILIENT, PYTHONIC ORCHESTRATION AND MCP PLATFORMS -- PREFECT OSS https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect, PREFECT CLOUD https://www.prefect.io/prefect/cloud, FASTMCP OSS, https://gofastmcp.com/getting-started/welcome AND HORIZON https://www.prefect.io/horizon-- USED FOR MISSION-CRITICAL WORKLOADS.






Our Vision: Prefect will define automation for the context era.

Our Mission: Curate an intelligent context layer that delivers the right information at the right time.


Our remote first company is singularly focused on this vision and mission, and every team member directly contributes to their advancement.

We've carefully created a supportive, high-performance culture - the operating system of our company - that empowers our team to do the best work of their careers and achieve their personal and professional aspirations.

Our company operates both open-source and commercial offerings, partnering with Fortune 500 companies, data innovators, and fast-growing digitally native startups. We are looking for folks who want to join a remote-first team #LI-Remote to continue building an equally amazing company, product, and developer community.


ROLE SUMMARY:

We're looking for a UI-focused product engineer to join the Horizon team and own how users experience an enterprise-grade MCP gateway. Horizon helps teams deploy, govern, and scale MCP servers. The buyers showing up today are security-minded and platform-minded enterprises who care about access control, auditability, and keeping sensitive data safe.

This is a UI-focused, front-end-leaning full-stack role. Your strength is the product surface: deep front-end craft plus strong opinions about how users interact with the product: the user flows and information architecture, the web app experience, and the framework and underlying technologies. We're explicitly not hiring a stylist or a pure UX researcher. We're hiring an engineer who understands the underlying technology well enough to guide AI tools rather than rely on them for technical judgment, and who can turn complex governance concepts into something legible and intuitive.

You'll own features end-to-end. from the user problem, to the flow and information architecture, to a polished, shipped experience backed by solid full-stack implementation. And you'll be a thought partner to the rest of the Horizon team: we're deliberately hiring two engineers into this profile so the product surface is never owned by someone working alone — you'll bounce ideas around, reinforce hypotheses, and set the bar for how Horizon feels.

You'll report to Eddie (Edward Park), Director of Engineering for Horizon, and partner directly with our CEO, GtM partners, and early customers to turn field signal into working software fast.



WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Own end-to-end UI and product features in Horizon — from user problem to information architecture to shipped, polished experience. You define what "done" looks like.

 - Set the bar for how users interact with Horizon — the web app experience, the flows, and how complex governance concepts are made legible.

 - Bring deep front-end craft — TypeScript, React, and the underlying web technologies — and the judgment to direct AI development tools rather than depend on them.

 - Be a thought partner to the team — collaborate closely so product and UX direction is a shared conversation, not a solo burden.

 - Translate enterprise signal into product — partner with GTM and customers to turn the "enterprise-legible" story (gateway plugins, PII filtering, audit logs, SCIM) into experiences buyers immediately understand.

 - Write maintainable, performant full-stack code — your center of gravity is the product surface, and you're capable across the stack when the feature demands it.

 - Develop deep product knowledge of the MCP ecosystem and AI/LLM tooling space.

 - Participate in on-call / incident response and help maintain production reliability standa

source_jobs.title: Account Executive (Technical Sales)
source_jobs.description:
PREFECT https://www.prefect.io/ BUILDS AND OPERATES RESILIENT, PYTHONIC ORCHESTRATION AND MCP PLATFORMS -- PREFECT OSS https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect, PREFECT CLOUD https://www.prefect.io/prefect/cloud, FASTMCP OSS, https://gofastmcp.com/getting-started/welcome AND HORIZON https://www.prefect.io/horizon-- USED FOR MISSION-CRITICAL WORKLOADS.






Our Vision: Prefect will define automation for the context era.

Our Mission: Curate an intelligent context layer that delivers the right information at the right time.


Our remote first company is singularly focused on this vision and mission, and every team member directly contributes to their advancement.

We've carefully created a supportive, high-performance culture - the operating system of our company - that empowers our team to do the best work of their careers and achieve their personal and professional aspirations.

Our company operates both open-source and commercial offerings, partnering with Fortune 500 companies, data innovators, and fast-growing digitally native startups. We are looking for folks who want to join a remote-first team #LI-Remote to continue building an equally amazing company, product, and developer community.



ROLE SUMMARY:

We are looking for an Account Executive (Technical Sales) to join our team. As an experience “hunter”, you will own and grow a book of business focused on net-new Prefect opportunities. You’ll combine product-led sales with targeted outbound to build pipeline, and partner closely with Sales Engineers pre-sale.

We’re looking for someone with a proven track record of closing six-figure deals with developer SaaS. Your mission is to meet or exceed revenue targets by landing new logos, running consultative evaluations, and translating technical adoption into business value. This is a high-impact role at the center of our go-to-market engine, ideal for a self-starter who thrives in remote-first, high-ambiguity environments

You'll report to Prefect's Director of Sales, Shane.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Own the full sales cycle for new logos—from prospecting and qualifying through discovery, evaluation, negotiation, and close.

 - Generate pipeline by blending inbound signals (OSS adoption, PQLs, self-serve Cloud users) with creative outbound; use Prefect’s brand awareness and market momentum to create new lead generation playbooks and channels; maintain ≥3× pipeline coverage quarter over quarter.

 - Run technical evaluations with Sales Engineers, setting success criteria, designing evaluation plans, and ensuring clear roles and responsibilities in every deal.

 - Deliver crisp handoffs to Solutions Engineers and CSMs after close, sharing goals, architecture notes, and risks to set customers up for adoption, renewals, and expansion.

 - Operate with rigor: maintain stage exit criteria, deliver accurate forecasts, keep CRM notes clean, and run disciplined close plans.

 - Share market insights with Product and Sales leadership to inform roadmap and GTM; contribute repeatable positioning, talk tracks, and case studies.




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS:

 - 2–10+ years in full-cycle AE roles at developer-tool, data-infra, or OSS companies with consistent 100%+ attainment and net-new logo wins

 - Proven success closing $50k–$250k ACV deals, including designing/running technical evaluations, org mapping, and multi-threading in account.

 - Comfortable partnering with Sales Engineers pre-sale and handing off to Solutions Engineers + CSMs post-sale in a triad model.

 - Strength in product-led sales environments: converting PQLs, OSS adopters, and self-serve users into paying customers and early expansions.

 - High EQ and strong collaboration skills: able to navigate technical and executive audiences, build trust quickly, and write clearly.

 - Startup-ready: thrives in a “low O₂” environment with light process, high ambiguity, and a builder’s mindset.



WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN:

Base Salary Ranges:

 - San Francisco Bay Area & NYC Metro: $14

source_jobs.title: Developer Events Manager
source_jobs.description:

Our Vision: Prefect will define automation for the context era.

Our Mission: Curate an intelligent context layer that delivers the right information at the right time.


Our remote first company is singularly focused on this vision and mission, and every team member directly contributes to their advancement.

We've carefully created a supportive, high-performance culture - the operating system of our company - that empowers our team to do the best work of their careers and achieve their personal and professional aspirations.

Our company operates both open-source and commercial offerings, partnering with Fortune 500 companies, data innovators, and fast-growing digitally native startups. We are looking for folks who want to join a remote-first team #LI-Remote to continue building an equally amazing company, product, and developer community.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Drive Prefect's event strategy from conception to execution, converting our developer communities into commercial revenue through authentic technical relationships. You'll own the full lifecycle of our event presence - from identifying the right conferences to attend to designing world-class developer-focused experiences that pull data engineers, MCP/Context engineers, and AI/ML engineers into our ecosystem. Build the PyAI brand through strategic community engagement while leveraging Prefect's physical spaces to increase community and customer impact that drives our product-led sales motion.



Strategic Event Management:

 - Develop and execute comprehensive event strategies across all Prefect products, including program mix, calendar planning, and audience targeting

 - Partner directly with leadership team to ensure alignment on event priorities and presence

 - Own budget planning, ROI measurement, and performance reporting for all event initiatives


Event Execution:

 - Lead execution of hosted and sponsored events across global markets - managing logistics, budgets, vendors, and onsite operations

 - Work cross-functionally to coordinate speaker preparation, booth presence, and attendee engagement

 - Design experiences that showcase Prefect's platforms to data engineers, ML engineers, and other developers

 - Manage multiple events simultaneously, attending key flagship events while remotely coordinating logistics for others

 - Own end-to-end execution of customer advisory events in partnership with product and sales teams


Community & Program Leadership:

 - Launch and manage online community initiatives including forums, user groups, and ambassador programs

 - Build contributor programs that engage technical practitioners in content creation and open source participation

 - Create engagement strategies that bridge open source users with enterprise customer opportunities

 - Oversee full event lifecycle from driving registrations to post-event lead nurturing across all developer events
   
   


YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

Required Experience:

 - 5+ years managing developer communities and events, with progression from execution to strategic leadership

 - Background in developer advocacy, developer relations, or technical community building—you're someone who can credibly engage with engineers, introduce technical speakers, and hold your own in conversations about Python, data infrastructure, or AI tooling

 - Proven track record launching and scaling community programs (forums, user groups, ambassador programs)

 - Experience working with technical audiences in infrastructure, DevOps, API, or data engineering spaces

 - Strong metrics-driven approach to community growth and engagement measurement

 - Background co

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer (Fullstack, Cloud)
source_jobs.description:
PREFECT https://www.prefect.io/ BUILDS AND OPERATES RESILIENT, PYTHONIC ORCHESTRATION AND MCP PLATFORMS -- PREFECT OSS https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect, PREFECT CLOUD https://www.prefect.io/prefect/cloud, FASTMCP OSS, https://gofastmcp.com/getting-started/welcome AND HORIZON https://www.prefect.io/horizon-- USED FOR MISSION-CRITICAL WORKLOADS.






Our Vision: Prefect will define automation for the context era.

Our Mission: Curate an intelligent context layer that delivers the right information at the right time.


Our remote first company is singularly focused on this vision and mission, and every team member directly contributes to their advancement.

We've carefully created a supportive, high-performance culture - the operating system of our company - that empowers our team to do the best work of their careers and achieve their personal and professional aspirations.

Our company operates both open-source and commercial offerings, partnering with Fortune 500 companies, data innovators, and fast-growing digitally native startups. We are looking for folks who want to join a remote-first team #LI-Remote to continue building an equally amazing company, product, and developer community.


ROLE SUMMARY:

We're looking for a full-stack engineer who leans front-end to join the Prefect Cloud team at a pivotal moment. Prefect Cloud is moving into agentic workflows — letting people describe the outcomes they want agents to achieve and then run, schedule, observe, and govern those agents with the same orchestration backbone Prefect is known for. You'll help define the UX of that new surface: execution plans, no-code / natural-language agent authoring, human-in-the-loop gates, and seamless integration with the broader developer ecosystem.

We strongly prefer someone who brings knowledge of the orchestration and developer-tools space.

You'll report to Zach (Zach Angell), Director of Engineering for Prefect Cloud, and partner directly with our product leadership, GtM partners, and early customers to turn field signal into working software fast.


WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Key team member who shapes and ships the agentic-workflow product surface in Prefect Cloud — execution plans, agent authoring, human-in-the-loop gates — from problem to polished, shipped experience.

 - Own user-facing features end-to-end across the stack, with your center of gravity on the front-end.

 - Be a thought partner to the Prefect Cloud engineering team on product and UX direction.

 - Bring orchestration / dev-tools intuition to product decisions — you understand the user and the category.

 - Partner with customers and GTM, including design partners, to turn signal into working software with a fast feedback loops.

 - Write maintainable, performant full-stack code and participate in on-call / incident response.


YOUR QUALIFICATIONS:

 - 5+ years of full-stack engineering experience with demonstrated success shipping user-facing products.

 - Strong front-end skills: deep proficiency in TypeScript, Vue, and modern UI libraries. You can go from idea to polished implementation and have opinions about what makes good UX.

 - Track record of shipping in high-ambiguity, early-stage environments without a dedicated PM.

 - Comfortable across the full stack; your strength and energy are on the product surface.

 - Knowledge of the orchestration / workflow / developer-tools space strongly preferred — e.g. experience at or with tools like Temporal, Airflow, Langsmith, n8n or similar. Adjacent dev-tools / dev-platform experience is also valuable.

 - Genuine interest in agents and AI/LLM tooling; you follow where the space is heading and have opinions.

 - Real fluency with AI development tools. You've shipped meaningful work with Claude Code, Codex, or their equivalents. You stay accountable for what they produce: you direct them with specs legible to humans and machines; and use them to create a high trust production environment for users.


WHAT YOU'LL GET IN RETURN

source_jobs.title: Senior Sales Engineer
source_jobs.description:
PREFECT https://www.prefect.io/ BUILDS AND OPERATES RESILIENT, PYTHONIC ORCHESTRATION AND MCP PLATFORMS -- PREFECT OSS https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect, PREFECT CLOUD https://www.prefect.io/prefect/cloud, FASTMCP OSS, https://gofastmcp.com/getting-started/welcome AND HORIZON https://www.prefect.io/horizon-- USED FOR MISSION-CRITICAL WORKLOADS.






Our Vision: Prefect will define automation for the context era.

Our Mission: Curate an intelligent context layer that delivers the right information at the right time.


Our remote first company is singularly focused on this vision and mission, and every team member directly contributes to their advancement.

We've carefully created a supportive, high-performance culture - the operating system of our company - that empowers our team to do the best work of their careers and achieve their personal and professional aspirations.

Our company operates both open-source and commercial offerings, partnering with Fortune 500 companies, data innovators, and fast-growing digitally native startups. We are looking for folks who want to join a remote-first team #LI-Remote to continue building an equally amazing company, product, and developer community.


ROLE SUMMARY:

As a Senior Sales Engineer on the Go-to-Market team at Prefect, you will own the technical relationship with our most strategic customers and prospects. You will lead complex evaluations end-to-end — from discovery through architecture review, POC, and production deployment — partnering directly with platform engineering, data, and ML teams at enterprise organizations.

Our customers increasingly span both traditional data engineering and AI-native use cases — deploying agents, integrating MCP servers, and building durable autonomous workflows. You'll help them architect solutions across the full surface of Prefect's platform.

We expect our Senior Sales Engineer to operate with autonomy and ownership — shaping the SE playbook as much as running it. You will develop repeatable methodologies, contribute to product direction through customer signal, and help scale the GTM function beyond yourself.

You will report to George Coyne, Director of Engineering, GTM.



EXPECTATIONS (YOU WILL):

 - Develop a clear, technical understanding of Prefect's platform — including durable execution, AI agent orchestration, MCP, and the broader ecosystem of competing solutions

 - Lead complex, multi-stakeholder technical evaluations with senior engineering stakeholders across extended enterprise sales cycles

 - Build and support long-lasting, mutually beneficial relationships with enterprise clients

 - Present and demonstrate the value of Prefect from a technical perspective to prospective clients

 - Help bring in new business to Prefect, achieving monthly revenue goals

 - Shape product roadmap by synthesizing patterns across customer engagements into clear, actionable feedback for product and engineering

 - Mentor and elevate other members of the GTM team through deal collaboration, technical coaching, and knowledge sharing

 - Champion our culture and foster a collaborative work environment

 - Embody ownership: contribute and advocate for your ideas, while also preventing projects from failing



WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Engage directly with customers to design, develop, and deploy solutions using Prefect Cloud

 - Build production-grade demos, reference architectures, and proof-of-concept deployments that demonstrate Prefect's value in a prospect's actual environment

 - Drive competitive displacement from legacy orchestration tools (Airflow, Dagster, Step Functions, Luigi) by articulating Prefect's technical differentiation

 - Develop and deliver technical content — blog posts, webinars, tutorials, and conference talks — that builds Prefect's credibility in the data and AI engineering community

 - Help customers design and deploy AI-native workflows — including agent orchestration, MCP tool use, and durable execution patterns for auton

notes: Prefect is a remote-first company with a strong focus on engineering and product development.
